page_title: Shrinkify — Make Your Media Library Breathe Again
hero_p: Shrinkify converts old H.264 videos and JPEG photos to modern H.265 and AVIF formats — cutting your library size by up to 60% without losing a single pixel of quality.
how_p: does the technical heavy lifting so you don't have to know what H.265 means.
step1_p: Drop in your Google Takeout export, camera roll backup, or any folder full of photos and videos.
step2_p: reads every file's codec, bitrate, and format. Finds duplicates. Shows a detailed savings report before touching anything.
step3_p: Converted files land in a shrinkified/ folder. Originals are untouched. If a conversion makes a file bigger, it's discarded.
feat_p: Built for everyday people and power users alike.
feat1_p: Re-encodes old MP4, MOV, AVI, and MKV files to HEVC — the same quality, half the size. Uses your GPU automatically on Apple Silicon, NVIDIA, Intel, and AMD hardware.
feat2_p: Converts JPEG photos to the modern AVIF format — royalty-free, better compression than HEIF, and fully supported on all modern devices. EXIF metadata, GPS location, and date are fully preserved via exiftool.
feat3_p: Finds files that are identical regardless of filename using fast file hashing. Shows you exactly which files are duplicates and lets you delete them safely.
feat4_p_post: generates a detailed report showing codec distribution, estimated savings breakdown, and a list of every file — organized into categories.
feat5_p: Enable "Preserve folder structure" and your subfolder hierarchy is mirrored inside the output folder. Your album organization stays intact.
feat6_p: Every converted file goes into a shrinkified/ subfolder. Your source files are read-only. Dry run mode lets you preview results before committing.
results_p: Actual results from a real Google Takeout export.
dl_p: Download the right version for your operating system. All builds are produced automatically by GitHub Actions.
dl_win_p: Windows 10 or later. Installer-free — just download and run.
dl_mac_p: macOS 12 or later. Apple Silicon native. GPU acceleration via VideoToolbox.
dl_linux_p: Ubuntu 22.04+. Run the binary directly. May require chmod +x Shrinkify.
dl_mobile_p: Mobile support is not currently available. We may explore iOS and Android builds in a future release as the project matures.
dl_note: ⚠ Requires ffmpeg — Shrinkify uses ffmpeg for video analysis and conversion. Install it from ffmpeg.org and make sure it's in your system PATH. On macOS: brew install ffmpeg. On Linux: sudo apt install ffmpeg.
guide_f1_p: The welcome screen lets you choose between a single file or an entire folder.
guide_f2_h3: Select your folder and click Analyze
guide_f2_p: Shrinkify scans every file, finds duplicates, and shows an estimated savings report.
guide_f3_p: Check "Dry run" to preview what would happen without touching any files.
guide_f4_p: Converted files appear in a shrinkified/ subfolder. Originals untouched.
guide_s1_p: Perfect for converting one video or photo without scanning a whole folder.
guide_s2_p: Supports MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV, JPG, WEBP, AVIF, and more. PNG files are scanned but not converted (lossless format).
guide_s3_p: Balanced is the recommended default. Maximum Shrink gives the smallest file. Conservative is safest for archival.
guide_s4_p: The converted file lands next to the original in a shrinkified/ folder.
compat_h3: Will the converted files open on my devices?
compat_p: H.265 and AVIF are modern standards supported on all devices from roughly 2018 onwards. Here's a quick reference before you convert your whole library.
compat_good_1: Windows 10 / 11 (free HEVC extension from Microsoft Store)
compat_bad_1: Windows 7 / 8 — no native H.265 support; AVIF requires a modern viewer
compat_bad_2: Android 8 and older — inconsistent hardware support
compat_footer: If you share files with people on older devices, keep your originals. Shrinkify never deletes them.
footer_desc: Analyze media files, suggest codec conversions, detect duplicates, and generate a detailed HTML report. Free and open source, forever.
footer_collab: 🤝 Built in collaboration with Claude (Anthropic)
